algorithms.title: Sepsis Recognition Sequence
algorithms.description: Identifying and responding to suspected sepsis, especially in elderly patients.
steps: Screen for fever and altered level of consciousness in any at-risk patient.
Consider atypical presentations in the elderly: falls, generalized weakness, or new confusion without a robust fever.
Actively search for a source: lungs, urinary tract (especially with an indwelling catheter), skin/wounds, abdomen.
Assess for progression toward distributive shock: tachycardia, tachypnea, hypotension.
Support ABCs, provide fluids per protocol, keep the patient warm, and transport promptly for source identification and treatment.

algorithms.title: Anaphylaxis Recognition and Treatment
algorithms.description: Rapid recognition and epinephrine-first management of anaphylaxis.
steps: Identify a known or suspected allergen exposure with a systemic reaction.
Look for the most diagnostic findings: hypotension and/or airway swelling (stridor, angioedema).
Give IM epinephrine immediately as first-line treatment — do not delay for other medications.
Add antihistamines and corticosteroids as adjuncts only, after epinephrine.
Manage the airway, support blood pressure with fluids as needed, and reassess frequently for recurrence or worsening.
Transport promptly given the risk of biphasic or progressive reactions.
